text: Continuous integration is a software development practice where team members frequently merge their code changes into a shared repository. Each time code is added, automated builds are triggered to compile the project. After that, running tests automatically verifies that the new code does not break existing functionality. This process helps in catching issues early, often within minutes of a change being made. CI pipelines manage this entire workflow, from code submission to build and test, without human intervention. By identifying problems quickly, developers can fix them before they become larger, saving time and improving software quality.
